message:
feat(core): adaptive poll delay, snapshot trigger tests, mux_client improvements

runtime.rs:
- Simplify observation loop poll match with if-let replacing
  multi-arm match (Ok(Some) vs Ok(None)|Err collapsed)
- Use mpsc_recv_option() wrapper for event channel receive to
  ensure asupersync-runtime compatibility
- Add #[allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_ref_mut)] on watch helper
- Add 3 new snapshot_trigger_from_event tests: PatternDetected,
  PaneDiscovered/PaneDisappeared lifecycle events, and negative
  cases (SegmentCaptured, GapDetected, WorkflowStarted, WorkflowStep)

vendored/mux_client.rs (+285 lines):
- Add pane_delta_try_send() non-blocking send helper that returns
  success boolean, replacing fallible try_send + async gap send
  with synchronous gap emission on backpressure
- Add subscription_poll_delay() function implementing adaptive
  polling: uses min_poll_interval when dirty output was seen (fast
  path), falls back to normal poll_interval otherwise
- Track saw_dirty_output through the poll result match arms to
  feed into adaptive delay
- Add #[allow(clippy::single_range_in_vec_init)] on test module
- Add test: subscription_poll_delay_uses_fast_path_when_dirty

storage.rs:
- Replace crate::runtime_compat::join! with futures::future::join
  for concurrent read test (avoids macro expansion issues under
  asupersync-runtime feature)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
